is governed by the [Namibia Football Association](/wiki/Namibia_Football_Association "Namibia Football Association").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The [Namibia Premier Football League](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_Football_League "Namibia Premier Football League") is the main domestic league. The [Namibia national football team](/wiki/Namibia_national_football_team "Namibia national football team") has never qualified for the [FIFA World Cup](/wiki/FIFA_World_Cup "FIFA World Cup"), but has twice been runner up in the [COSAFA Cup](/wiki/COSAFA_Cup "COSAFA Cup").[[2]](#cite_note-2) They qualified for two Africa Cups of Nations, in [1998](/wiki/1998_African_Cup_of_Nations "1998 African Cup of Nations") and [2008](/wiki/2008_Africa_Cup_of_Nations "2008 Africa Cup of Nations"), being eliminated in the first round both times.[[3]](#cite_note-3) [Association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") is the most popular sport in Namibia, followed by [rugby union](/wiki/Rugby_union "Rugby union").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

**Football in Namibia** is governed by the [Namibia Football Association](/wiki/Namibia_Football_Association "Namibia Football Association").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The [Namibia Premier Football League](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_Football_League "Namibia Premier Football League") is the main domestic league. The [Namibia national football team](/wiki/Namibia_national_football_team "Namibia national football team") has never qualified for the [FIFA World Cup](/wiki/FIFA_World_Cup "FIFA World Cup"), but has twice been runner up in the [COSAFA Cup](/wiki/COSAFA_Cup "COSAFA Cup").[[2]](#cite_note-2) They qualified for two Africa Cups of Nations, in [1998](/wiki/1998_African_Cup_of_Nations "1998 African Cup of Nations") and [2008](/wiki/2008_Africa_Cup_of_Nations "2008 Africa Cup of Nations"), being eliminated in the first round both times.[[3]](#cite_note-3) [Association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") is the most popular sport in Namibia, followed by [rugby union](/wiki/Rugby_union "Rugby union").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## 2008 Africa Cup of Nations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Football_in_Namibia&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: 2008 Africa Cup of Nations")]

In September 2007, the national team qualified for just their second ever African Cup of Nations appearance, which took place in [Ghana](/wiki/Ghana "Ghana"). The Namibian squad was defeated 5–1 by [Morocco](/wiki/Morocco_national_football_team "Morocco national football team") in their first game, 0–1 by host [Ghana](/wiki/Ghana_national_football_team "Ghana national football team") in their 2nd and tied [Guinea](/wiki/Guinea_national_football_team "Guinea national football team") one all and thus did not earn a spot in the quarterfinal round.
